prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿跳" (straight-leg hops) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-air during stiff-legged pogo hops: both legs nearly fully extended (only minimal knee bend) and parallel to each other; ankles plantarflexed (toes pointed down) at takeoff; body rigid like a pogo stick; arms held at sides slightly bent at the elbows for balance; torso upright; reactive ankle bouncing drill emphasizing calf muscle elasticity; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and ankle stiffness. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadriceps and core muscles for body rigidity. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌 / 比目鱼肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
